metadata.name: PeriGen Security Questionnaire KB
metadata.description: Base knowledge for autofilling security questionnaires and learning new Q→A pairs.

pattern_rules.answer: Yes. Client–server communication uses HTTPS over TLS 1.2+ with customer-provided certificates.
Customer-managed. SQL Server Transparent Data Encryption (TDE) is fully supported where enabled.
Yes. Active Directory LDAP/LDAPS is supported. Entra ID (Azure AD) SSO is supported for HUB when configured.
Yes. Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) is supported and used to restrict privileges.
Yes. Authentication and access events are logged centrally and can be exported to SIEM solutions.
Yes. HL7 ADT/ORU interfaces are supported via the customer interface engine with optional channel encryption.
Vendor support uses SecureLink with named accounts; VPN is not required for standard support.
Yes. Client components can run in Citrix/VDI environments. Packaging via SCCM/Intune is supported.
Microsoft SQL Server 2019/2022 (customer-managed). Application DBs require db_owner; sysadmin is not required.
Customer-managed backups. VMware HA/vMotion supported. SQL Always On/Clustering optional based on site policy.
PeriGen does not host PHI/PII. All data remains on customer on-prem servers.
Mobile access is governed by Microsoft 365 MDM policies. Remote wipe/disable is supported. BYOD allowed only with enforced controls. ePHI is never transmitted via SMS.
Yes. Static application security testing (SAST) is performed regularly with remediation of critical/high issues before release. Development follows secure SDLC aligned with OWASP practices.
No internet-facing components are required. Standard deployments do not require DMZ placement.
